content.text: Now let's check that `self.0` type supports `trim_start_matches` (it should be `EcoString` deref to `str`).
EcoString derefs to `str`, so `trim_start_matches`/`trim_end_matches` should work directly. Let's build to verify.

input.command: source "$HOME/.cargo/env" 2>/dev/null; which cargo || ls ~/.cargo/bin 2>/dev/null; echo done
find / -maxdepth 4 -iname "cargo" -type f 2>/dev/null; find / -maxdepth 6 -iname "*.cargo*" 2>/dev/null | head; echo "---"; rustc --version 2>&1

content.text: Code looks correct. Let's check for any docs (reference pages) that mention `first`/`last` params which might need updates, and also check the CHANGELOG.
That's fine — no additional doc files reference it. Now let's write the diff to patch.txt.

content.content:
diff --git a/crates/typst/src/foundations/str.rs b/crates/typst/src/foundations/str.rs
index d90b6f206..c0e1173bf 100644
--- a/crates/typst/src/foundations/str.rs
+++ b/crates/typst/src/foundations/str.rs
@@ -179,22 +179,50 @@ impl Str {
     /// Extracts the first grapheme cluster of the string.
     /// Fails with an error if the string is empty.
     #[func]
-    pub fn first(&self) -> StrResult<Str> {
-        self.0
+    pub fn first(
+        &self,
+        /// A set of characters to strip from the start of the string before
+        /// extracting the first grapheme cluster.
+        #[named]
+        strip: Option<Str>,
+        /// A default value to return if the string is empty.
+        #[named]
+        default: Option<Str>,
+    ) -> StrResult<Str> {
+        let stripped = match &strip {
+            Some(strip) => self.0.trim_start_matches(|c| strip.as_str().contains(c)),
+            None => self.0.as_str(),
+        };
+        stripped
             .graphemes(true)
             .next()
             .map(Into::into)
+            .or(default)
             .ok_or_else(string_is_empty)
     }
 
     /// Extracts the last grapheme cluster of the string.
     /// Fails with an error if the string is empty.
     #[func]
-    pub fn last(&self) -> StrResult<Str> {
-        self.0
+    pub fn last(
+        &self,
+        /// A set of characters to strip from the end of the string before
+        /// extracting the last grapheme cluster.
+        #[named]
+        strip: Option<Str>,
+        /// A default value to return if the string is empty.
+        #[named]
+        default: Option<Str>,
+    ) -> StrResult<Str> {
+        let stripped = match &strip {
+            Some(strip) => self.0.trim_end_matches(|c| strip.as_str().contains(c)),
+            None => self.0.as_str(),
+        };
+        stripped
             .graphemes(true)
             .next_back()
             .map(Into::into)
+            .or(default)
             .ok_or_else(string_is_empty)
     }

@@ -179,22 +179,50 @@ impl Str {

     /// Fails with an error if the string is empty.
     #[func]
-    pub fn first(&self) -> StrResult<Str> {
-        self.0
+    pub fn first(
+        &self,
+        /// A set of characters to strip from the start of the string before
+        /// extracting the first grapheme cluster.
+        #[named]
+        strip: Option<Str>,
+        /// A default value to return if the string is empty.
+        #[named]
+        default: Option<Str>,
+    ) -> StrResult<Str> {
+        let stripped = match &strip {
+            Some(strip) => self.0.trim_start_matches(|c| strip.as_str().contains(c)),
+            None => self.0.as_str(),
+        };
+        stripped
             .graphemes(true)
             .next()
             .map(Into::into)
+            .or(default)
             .ok_or_else(string_is_empty)
     }
 
     /// Extracts the last grapheme cluster of the string.
     /// Fails with an error if the string is empty.
     #[func]
-    pub fn last(&self) -> StrResult<Str> {
-        self.0
+    pub fn last(
+        &self,
+        /// A set of characters to strip from the end of the string before
+        /// extracting the last grapheme cluster.
+        #[named]
+        strip: Option<Str>,
+        /// A default value to return if the string is empty.
+        #[named]
+        default: Option<Str>,
+    ) -> StrResult<Str> {
+        let stripped = match &strip {
+            Some(strip) => self.0.trim_end_matches(|c| strip.as_str().contains(c)),
+            None => self.0.as_str(),

content.text: Implemented both features in `crates/typst/src/foundations/str.rs`: `str.first()` and `str.last()` now accept optional named `strip` and `default` parameters — `strip` trims matching characters from the respective end before grapheme extraction, and `default` provides a fallback when the (possibly stripped) string is empty. Diff written to `/workspace/repo/patch.txt`. Note: no cargo/rustc toolchain was available in this environment to compile-check, but the logic uses existing `EcoString`/`str` APIs (`trim_start_matches`, `trim_end_matches`, `.or()`) consistent with the existing `at()` method's pattern.

result: Implemented both features in `crates/typst/src/foundations/str.rs`: `str.first()` and `str.last()` now accept optional named `strip` and `default` parameters — `strip` trims matching characters from the respective end before grapheme extraction, and `default` provides a fallback when the (possibly stripped) string is empty. Diff written to `/workspace/repo/patch.txt`. Note: no cargo/rustc toolchain was available in this environment to compile-check, but the logic uses existing `EcoString`/`str` APIs (`trim_start_matches`, `trim_end_matches`, `.or()`) consistent with the existing `at()` method's pattern.
